threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: Seeing someone pull out a paper map during the tube strike is a clear sign. Locals just know the way around the chaos.
Caught the 59 last night, and a guy was trying to sell a parrot he'd trained to shout 'Brixton!' every time we hit a speed bump.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: It was the sound of a saxophonist jamming outside the Spätkauf at 2 AM, while a busker's cat lounged on the pavement, completely unfazed.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a Saturday market where a DJ spins vinyl while you sip local craft beer and munch on Turkish street food. That's Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name the exact bus line that gets delayed due to a dog on the tracks—like it’s just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo waiting on the Muni, clutching a bouquet—his bride was stuck on the train ahead. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume all local bars serve tapas but many still just do cafe amb llet. You'll miss some real charm if you only seek out the trendy spots.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot a recent arrival by the way they still believe they can get through Gràcia's labyrinthine streets without Google Maps.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just 'the train.' And don’t get me started on the journey time to Shoreditch.
Saw a guy on the 133 humming a whole opera. No one even batted an eyelid; just another night in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The way my neighbor’s cat started sunbathing on my windowsill, like she’s claiming her territory, made it feel like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a café that serves donuts with currywurst toppings. It's as bizarre as it sounds but somehow works.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can name the taco truck at 16th and Mission but can’t spell ‘BART’ without looking it up.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a guy holding a sax while waiting for a delayed Muni bus, crowd swaying to jazz as we all shared the misery. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists see the parks and skip the small plazas where locals hash out life over coffee. It’s where the real Gràcia spirit thrives.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still take photos of the street artists on every corner like it's a new theme park.
